steps.title: Set up the triangle perimeter
steps.content: The perimeter of a triangle is the sum of its three side lengths. Use $DE+DF+EF=19$ and substitute the two known side lengths.

steps.title: Set up the perimeter equation
steps.content: The perimeter of triangle DEF equals the sum of its three sides. With perimeter = 19 inches, DE = 5 inches, and DF = 8 inches, we write: $$DE + DF + EF = 19$$ Substituting the known values: $$5 + 8 + EF = 19$$

steps.title: Solve for EF
steps.content: Simplifying the equation: $$13 + EF = 19$$ Therefore: $$EF = 6$$ That matches Choice B — $6$.
